code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test23-APR-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, and elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), with low specific gravity. These findings indicate significant renal dysfunction, possible tubular damage, and metabolic derangements. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
